name: TRELLIS.2
description: Local TRELLIS.2 GGUF 3D generation backend packaged as an installable Nymph module.

overview.body:
TRELLIS.2 turns a source image into a high-fidelity textured 3D asset. It is Microsoft's open-source 4B image-to-3D model, using O-Voxel to handle complex shapes, sharp features, and PBR-style surface materials. This Nymph module installs a local GGUF backend for TRELLIS.2.

Model fetch guide:
- Install sets up the backend and Python runtime only. It does not download the large AI model files.
- Fetch Models downloads the selected Aero-Ex/Trellis2-GGUF quant bundle, shared vision/encoder/decoder files from that GGUF repo, the required microsoft/TRELLIS.2-4B support checkpoint, and the rembg u2net background-removal model.
- The selected quant is not one tiny file. It includes the GGUF pieces TRELLIS needs for shape/refiner generation, and the fetch script also allows matching texture GGUF files if the source repo publishes them.
- Start with Q5_K_M for most GPUs. It is the intended balance of quality, download size, and VRAM.
- Use Q4_K_M for the smallest first test or tighter VRAM. It is the quickest way to prove the module works.
- Use Q6_K if Q5 works and you want a heavier quality-focused option.
- Use Q8_0 only when you have plenty of VRAM and disk space. It is the largest option.
- All quants is optional. It downloads every supported quant so Blender/addon switching can choose later; shared files download once, but the GGUF portion becomes much larger.
- Model files are stored in $HOME/NymphsData/cache/huggingface and rembg u2net is stored in $HOME/NymphsData/models/rembg, not inside the disposable module repo.

FlashAttention install guide:
- Install may compile flash-attn from source with the official pip command.
- GPU chooses exactly one FLASH_ATTN_CUDA_ARCHS target for that build.
- Auto-detect uses the first supported NVIDIA compute capability it sees.
- If auto-detect cannot choose one target, install stops and asks for a manual GPU choice instead of compiling flash-attn's broad default arch list.
- Max jobs sets MAX_JOBS, the number of parallel build jobs.
- NVCC threads sets NVCC_THREADS inside each compiler job.
- For 32GB RAM, use Max jobs 4 and NVCC threads 2. Drop NVCC threads to 1 if memory pressure is high.

GPU arch quick guide:
- SM80: RTX 30-series, RTX 40-series, A100, A30, A10/A16/A2, A40, RTX A-series, L4, L40/L40S, and RTX Ada workstation GPUs.
- SM90: GH200, H200, H100, and H800.
- SM100: GB200, B200, GB300, and B300 datacenter Blackwell GPUs.
- SM110: Jetson Thor/T5000/T4000.
- SM120: GeForce RTX 50-series and RTX PRO Blackwell workstation/server GPUs.
requirements: Python 3.10 in the managed NymphsCore WSL distro
Enough free RAM for flash-attn native compilation

options.label: Auto-detect
options.description: Read the NVIDIA compute capability and select the flash-attn target arch

options.label: SM80 / RTX 30+40
options.description: 8.x GPUs: RTX 30/40, A100/A30/A10/A40, L4/L40, RTX A/Ada

options.label: SM90 / H100/H200
options.description: Hopper GPUs: GH200, H200, H100, H800

options.label: SM100 / B200
options.description: Datacenter Blackwell: GB200, B200, GB300, B300

options.label: SM110 / Jetson Thor
options.description: Jetson Thor targets: T5000 and T4000

options.label: SM120 / RTX 50
options.description: RTX 50-series and RTX PRO Blackwell workstation/server GPUs

options.label: MAX_JOBS=2
options.description: Lowest RAM pressure for the flash-attn source build

options.label: MAX_JOBS=4
options.description: Recommended flash-attn build cap for 32GB RAM

options.label: MAX_JOBS=8
options.description: More CPU and RAM use during the flash-attn source build

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=1
options.description: Safest compiler thread setting for lower RAM pressure

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=2
options.description: Moderate compiler threading if the machine has RAM headroom

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=4
options.description: Aggressive compiler threading; can multiply memory pressure with Max jobs

manager_action_groups.title: Model Fetch
manager_action_groups.description:
Install sets up the TRELLIS.2 GGUF backend only. Fetch Models downloads the actual model files.
Every fetch downloads shared GGUF support files, your selected quant bundle, the required microsoft/TRELLIS.2-4B support checkpoint, and rembg u2net.
Start with Q5_K_M for most GPUs. Q4_K_M is the smallest first test. Q6_K and Q8_0 are larger quality-focused options. Choose All quants only if Blender/addon switching needs every supported GGUF later.

options.label: All quants
options.description: Optional large download: every supported GGUF so Blender can switch later

options.label: Q4_K_M
options.description: Smallest download and lowest VRAM; best first proof test

options.label: Q5_K_M
options.description: Recommended balance of quality, download size, and VRAM

options.label: Q6_K
options.description: Heavier quality-focused option after Q5 works

options.label: Q8_0
options.description: Largest local GGUF option; use only with plenty of VRAM and disk

notes: This module preserves the main-branch GGUF quantized-model pull behavior.
Model fetch uses native manager_action_groups and installed module-owned scripts.
Do not commit GGUF model weights, Hugging Face cache, .venv, logs, outputs, or generated assets.
